title: Responses, Problem Details, Streaming, and Protocol-Aware Output · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective single-commit response and streaming contract; retain the failing request and response, isolate the first causal server boundary, and keep a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in single-commit response and streaming contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict implicit 200, content sniffing, automatic length, one final status, informational responses, and header commitment order.
Serialize RFC 9457 problem type, title, status, detail, instance, extensions, and application/problem+json without leaking implementation data.
Map validation, authentication, authorization, absence, conflict, precondition, limit, timeout, cancellation, dependency, and internal failures consistently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
